title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Speed Booster Test

questions.question: A thief seeing a policeman at a distance of 395 m started running at a speed of 6 km/h. The policeman immediately gave chase at a speed of 9 km/h and the thief was caught. What is the distance covered by the thief before being caught by the policeman?
questions.explanation: Given: Initial distance between thief and policeman = 395 m Speed of the thief = 6 km/h Speed of the policeman = 9 km/h Formula Used: Relative Speed = Speed of Policeman - Speed of Thief Time to catch = Initial DistanceRelative Speed \frac{Initial\ Distance}{Relative\ Speed}Relative SpeedInitial Distance‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Distance = Speed √ó Time Solution: Convert speeds from km/h to m/s by multiplying by 518:\frac{5}{18}:185‚Äã:‚Äã‚Äã Speed of thief = 6√ó518=53 m/s6 √ó \frac{5}{18} = \frac{5}{3} \text{ m/s}6√ó185‚Äã=35‚Äã m/s‚Äã‚Äã Speed of policeman = 9√ó518=52 m/s9 √ó \frac{5}{18} = \frac{5}{2} \text{ m/s}9√ó185‚Äã=25‚Äã m/s‚Äã‚Äã Calculate the relative speed of the policeman with respect to the thief: Relative speed = 52‚àí53 \frac{5}{2} - \frac{5}{3}25‚Äã‚àí35‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã =15‚àí106=56 m/s= \frac{15 - 10}{6} = \frac{5}{6} \text{ m/s}=615‚àí10‚Äã=65‚Äã m/s‚Äã‚Äã Calculate the time taken by the policeman to catch the thief: Time = 39556 \frac{395}{\frac{5}{6}}65‚Äã395‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã=395√ó65 = 395 √ó \frac{6}{5}=395√ó56‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã = 79 √ó 6 = 474 seconds Calculate the distance covered by the thief in this time: Distance =53 \frac{5}{3} 35‚Äã‚Äã√ó 474 = 5 √ó 158 = 790 m Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The area of a square is 3969 cm2cm^2cm2‚Äã. Its perimeter is equal to the perimeter of a regular hexagon. What is the area (in cm2)cm^2)cm2)‚Äã of the hexagon?
questions.explanation: Given Area of square = 3969 cm2cm^2cm2‚Äã‚Äã Perimeter of square = Perimeter of regular hexagon Formula Used Area of square = a2a^2a2‚Äã‚Äã Perimeter of square = 4a Perimeter of regular hexagon = 6s Area of regular hexagon = 332√ós2 \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} √ó s^2233‚Äã‚Äã√ós2‚Äã‚Äã Solution Let the side of the square be a. ‚Äãa2=3969a^2 = 3969a2=3969‚Äã‚Äã a = 63 cm Perimeter of the square = 4 √ó 63 = 252 cm Let the side of the regular hexagon be s. 6s = 252 s = 42 cm Area of the hexagon = 332√ó(42)2\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} √ó (42)^2233‚Äã‚Äã√ó(42)2‚Äã‚Äã ‚ÄãArea=332√ó1764 Area=33√ó882 Area=26463cm2 Area = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} √ó 1764\\\ \\Area = 3\sqrt{3} √ó 882\\\ \\Area = 2646\sqrt{3} cm^2\\\ \\Area=233‚Äã‚Äã√ó1764 Area=33‚Äã√ó882 Area=26463‚Äãcm2 ‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A bag contains 2 rupee, 1 rupee, fifty paise and twenty five paise coins in the proportion of 3:5:7:9. If the total amount is ‚Çπ 268 , find the number of 1 rupee coins in the bag.
questions.explanation: Given: Denominations = ‚Çπ 2, ‚Çπ 1, 50 paise (‚Çπ 0.50), 25 paise (‚Çπ 0.25) Ratio of coins = 3 : 5 : 7 : 9 Total Amount = ‚Çπ 268 Formula Used: Total Amount = Sum of (Number of coins √ó Value of each denomination) Solution: Let the number of ‚Çπ 2, ‚Çπ 1, 50p, and 25p coins be 3x, 5x, 7x, and 9x respectively. The total monetary value equation is: 2(3x) + 1(5x) + 0.50(7x) + 0.25(9x) = 268 6x + 5x + 3.5x + 2.25x = 268 16.75x = 268 x = 26816.75=268001675\frac{268}{16.75} = \frac{26800}{1675}16.75268‚Äã=167526800‚Äã‚Äã = 16 The number of 1 rupee coins is 5x: Number of 1 rupee coins = 5 √ó 16 = 80 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: At what rate (in percentage) per annum simple interest will a sum of money triple itself in 8 years?
questions.explanation: Given: Sum triples (becomes 3P) in 8 years Formula Used: SI = Amount - Principal = 3P - P = 2P Rate = SI√ó100P√óT\frac{SI \times 100}{P \times T}P√óTSI√ó100‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: R = 2P√ó100P√ó8\frac{2P \times 100}{P \times 8}P√ó82P√ó100‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã R = 2008=25% \frac{200}{8} = 25\%8200‚Äã=25%‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: If a gymnastic coach has 14 gymnasts, their heights are as follows (in cm): 172, 173, 164, 178, 168, 169, 173, 172, 173, 164, 178, 168, 169, 173 The mode of this data (in cm) is:
questions.explanation: Given Data set of heights (in cm): 172, 173, 164, 178, 168, 169, 173, 172, 173, 164, 178, 168, 169, 173 Concept Used Mode = The value that appears most frequently in a data set. Solution Let us count the frequency of each value in the given data set: 164 cm appears 2 times. 168 cm appears 2 times. 169 cm appears 2 times. 172 cm appears 2 times. 173 cm appears 4 times. 178 cm appears 2 times. Since 173 cm has the highest frequency (4 times), it is the mathematical mode of the data. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A retailer buys 1000 books for ‚Çπ70 each. He sells 300 books at profit of 30% and the remaining 700 books at a profit of 5%. What is the total profit (in ‚Çπ)?
questions.explanation: Given: Total books = 1000 Cost price per book = ‚Çπ70 Profit on 300 books = 30% Profit on 700 books = 5% Formula Used: Total Profit = Profit on first part + Profit on second part Profit =Cost Price√óProfit Percentage100 \frac{Cost\ Price √ó Profit\ Percentage}{100}100Cost Price√óProfit Percentage‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Cost price of 300 books = 300 √ó 70 = 21000. Profit on these 300 books = 21000 √ó 30100\frac{30}{100}10030‚Äã‚Äã = 6300 Cost price of remaining 700 books = 700 √ó 70 = 49000 Profit on these 700 books = 49000 √ó5100 \frac{5}{100}1005‚Äã‚Äã = 2450 Total Profit = 6300 + 2450 = 8750 The total profit is ‚Çπ8750 Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: In an election between two candidates, 80% of the registered voters cast their vote and 20% of the votes polled were found invalid. Winning candidate got 55% of the valid votes and won the election by a margin of 368 votes. How many voters were registered?
questions.explanation: Given Votes cast = 80% of registered voters Invalid votes = 20% of polled votes Winning candidate share = 55% of valid votes Winning margin = 368 votes Formula Used Valid Votes = Polled Votes - Invalid Votes Margin = (Winner % - Loser %) of Valid Votes Solution Let total registered voters be 100x. Votes polled = 80x Valid votes = 80x√ó80100=64x80x \times \frac{80}{100} = 64x80x√ó10080‚Äã=64x‚Äã‚Äã Winner's share = 55% of 64x Loser's share = (100 - 55)% = 45% of 64x Margin of victory = 55% - 45% = 10% of valid votes 10% of 64x = 368 ‚Äã10100√ó64x=368\frac{10}{100} \times 64x = 36810010‚Äã√ó64x=368‚Äã‚Äã 6.4x = 368 x = 3686.4=57.5 \frac{368}{6.4} = 57.56.4368‚Äã=57.5‚Äã‚Äã Total registered voters = 100x = 100√ó57.5=5750100 \times 57.5 = 5750100√ó57.5=5750‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/be03340d-0e7c-4a37-ac0d-859ead761ae1.png)

questions.question: Rawu can complete a piece of wob in 15 hours, Vinay in 20 hours and Vardhan in 30 hours. If they work together and they are paid Rs.7,200 for the wob, then what amount would Vinay receive?
questions.explanation: Given: Rawu can complete a piece of wob in 15 hours, Vinay in 20 hours Vardhan in 30 hours. Total earning from working together Rs. 7,200 for the wob Formula Used: Efficiency = Total Work/Total Time Solution: Total work = LCM of 15, 20 and 30 = 60 units Efficiency of Rawu = 60/15 = 4 units/hour Efficiency of Vinay = 60/20 = 3 units per hour Efficiency of Vardhan = 60/30 = 2 units/hour Total efficiency = 4 + 3 + 2 = 9 units/hour According to the question, => 7200 = 9 units => 1 unit = 72009\frac{7200}997200‚Äã‚Äã = 800 Vinay's efficiency => 3 units = 3 √ó 800 = 2400

questions.question: Ravi invested ‚Çπ8,000 at 20% per annum for 6 months. If the interest is compounded half yearly, what is the compound interest?
questions.explanation: Given: Principal (P) = ‚Çπ 8,000 Rate per annum = 20% Time = 6 months Compounding = Half-yearly Formula Used: Rate per half-year (R) = Annual Rate2\frac{Annual\ Rate}{2}2Annual Rate‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Time in half-years (n) = Months6\frac{Months}{6}6Months‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Amount (A) = P√ó(1+R100)nP √ó (1 + \frac{R}{100})^nP√ó(1+100R‚Äã)n‚Äã‚Äã Compound Interest (CI) = A - P Solution: Rate per half-year = 202\frac{20}{2}220‚Äã‚Äã = 10% Time in half-years = 66 \frac{6}{6}66‚Äã‚Äã = 1 half-year Amount = 8000 √ó (1 + 10100)1\frac{10}{100})^110010‚Äã)1‚Äã‚Äã Amount = 8000 √ó 110100\frac{110}{100} 100110‚Äã‚Äã= 8000 √ó 1.1 Amount = 8800 Compound Interest = 8800 - 8000 = 800 Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an item for ‚Çπ583.2 after giving two successive discounts of 25% and 40% on its marked price. Had he not given any discount, he would have earned a profit of 62%. What is the cost price (in ‚Çπ) of the item?
questions.explanation: Given: Final Selling Price = ‚Çπ583.2 Successive Discounts = 25% and 40% Profit without discount = 62% Formula Used: Selling Price = Marked Price √ó (1‚àíD1100)√ó(1‚àíD2100)(1 - \frac{D_1}{100}) √ó (1 - \frac{D_2}{100})(1‚àí100D1‚Äã‚Äã)√ó(1‚àí100D2‚Äã‚Äã)‚Äã‚Äã Cost Price = Selling Price1+Profit100\frac{Selling\ Price}{1 + \frac{Profit}{100}}1+100Profit‚ÄãSelling Price‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Let the Marked Price be M. M √ó(1‚àí25100)√ó(1‚àí40100) (1 - \frac{25}{100}) √ó (1 - \frac{40}{100})(1‚àí10025‚Äã)√ó(1‚àí10040‚Äã)‚Äã = 583.2 M √ó 0.75 √ó 0.60 = 583.2 M √ó 0.45 = 583.2 M = 583.20.45\frac{583.2}{0.45}0.45583.2‚Äã‚Äã = 1296 Had no discount been given, the Selling Price would be the Marked Price. New Selling Price = 1296 Given profit is 62%. CP √ó 1.62 = 1296 CP = 12961.62\frac{1296}{1.62}1.621296‚Äã‚Äã = 800 The cost price of the item is ‚Çπ800. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: The pie charts given below show percentage of expenditures of a family under different heads in the years 2021 and 2022. Under which of these heads did the percentage of expenditures increase from 2021 to 2022? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/3e3f88d6-f38f-42f3-9c47-96ab97750741.png)
questions.explanation: Given: Pie charts show expenditure percentages under five heads: Groceries, Child education, Medical, House rent, and Entertainment for 2021 and 2022. Step-by-step Comparison: ‚Ä¢ Groceries: 25% (2021) ‚Üí 20% (2022) ‚Üì ‚Ä¢ Child education: 15% (2021) ‚Üí 25% (2022) ‚Üë ‚Ä¢ Medical: 25% (2021) ‚Üí 15% (2022) ‚Üì ‚Ä¢ House rent: 15% (2021) ‚Üí 25% (2022) ‚Üë ‚Ä¢ Entertainment: 20% (2021) ‚Üí 15% (2022) ‚Üì The percentage of expenditures increased under the following heads: ‚Ä¢ Child education ‚Ä¢ House rent

questions.question: Study the following graph and answer the question that follows. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/0f2c6a88-4717-47fa-a350-3a417982fec8.png) Find the ratio of average sales of company P in 2012 to 2014 and the average sales of company Q in 2014 to 2016.
questions.explanation: Given: Sum of sales of company P (2012 to 2014); = 4.5 + 2.5 + 3.5 = 10.5 Sum of sales of company Q (2014 to 2016): = 1.9 + 2.9 + 4.2 = 9.0 Ratio = 10.5 : 9.0 = 7 : 6

questions.question: A chord AB of a circle is 30 cm long. If the perpendicular distance from the center to AB is 12 cm, calculate the radius of the circle. Also, determine the distance of another chord of length 24 cm from the center.
questions.explanation: Given Length of chord AB = 30 cm Perpendicular distance to AB = 12 cm Length of the second chord = 24 cm Formula Used A perpendicular drawn from the center to a chord bisects the chord. Pythagoras Theorem: Hypotenuse2=Base2+Perpendicular2\text{Hypotenuse}^2 = \text{Base}^2 + \text{Perpendicular}^2Hypotenuse2=Base2+Perpendicular2‚Äã‚Äã Solution ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/03d91a56-4d81-4f87-8dd6-c341f4b1cd12.png) ‚Äã The perpendicular from the center bisects chord AB. So, half of chord AB = 30√∑2=15 cm.30 \div 2 = 15 \text{ cm}.30√∑2=15 cm.‚Äã‚Äã Applying Pythagoras Theorem to find the radius (r): ‚Äãr2=152+122r2=225+144=369r=369‚âà19.2 cmr^2 = 15^2 + 12^2 \\r^2 = 225 + 144 = 369\\r = \sqrt{369} \approx 19.2 \text{ cm}r2=152+122r2=225+144=369r=369‚Äã‚âà19.2 cm‚Äã‚Äã Now, for the second chord of length 24 cm24 \text{ cm}24 cm‚Äã, half of its length = 24√∑2=12 cm.24 \div 2 = 12 \text{ cm}.24√∑2=12 cm.‚Äã‚Äã Let d be its perpendicular distance from the center. Using the same radius r: ‚Äãr2=d2+122369=d2+144d2=369‚àí144=225d=225=15 cmr^2 = d^2 + 12^2 \\369 = d^2 + 144\\d^2 = 369 - 144 = 225\\d = \sqrt{225} = 15 \text{ cm}r2=d2+122369=d2+144d2=369‚àí144=225d=225‚Äã=15 cm‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: 20% of A's income is equal to 35% of 'B', and 15% of 'B' is equal to 40% of 'C'. If the income of 'C' is ‚Çπ 30,000, then what is the total income of A, B and C?
questions.explanation: Given: 20% of A = 35% of B 15% of B = 40% of C Income of C = ‚Çπ 30,000 Solution: Start by finding the income of B using the given relationship with C: 0.15B = 0.40C Substitute the value of C (30000): 0.15B = 0.40 √ó 30000 0.15B = 12000 B = 120000.15\frac{12000}{0.15}0.1512000‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã B = 80000 Now, use the income of B to find the income of A: 0.20A = 0.35B Substitute the value of B (80000): 0.20A = 0.35 √ó 80000 0.20A = 28000 A = 280000.20\frac{28000}{0.20}0.2028000‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã A = 140000 Finally, calculate the total income of A, B, and C: Total Income = A + B + C Total Income = 140000 + 80000 + 30000 Total Income = 250000 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Let ABCD be a quadrilateral which satisfies any of the following conditions. ( i ) opposite sides are equal ( ii ) each diagonal of ABCD divides it into congruent triangles ( iii ) each pair of opposite angles are equal ( iv ) diagonals of ABCD bisect each other Then ABCD has to be a _____________
questions.explanation: Solution: (i) Opposite sides are equal: If AB = CD and BC = AD, then by woining diagonal AC, triangles ABC and CDA are congruent (SSS). Hence, corresponding angles are equal, which implies opposite sides are parallel. Therefore, ABCD is a parallelogram. (ii) Each diagonal divides it into congruent triangles: If a diagonal divides the quadrilateral into two congruent triangles, then corresponding sides are equal. This reduces to condition (i), so ABCD is a parallelogram. (iii) Each pair of opposite angles are equal: If ‚àÝA = ‚àÝC and ‚àÝB = ‚àÝD, then using the angle sum property (360¬∞), we get consecutive angles supplementary. Thus, opposite sides are parallel, and ABCD is a parallelogram. (iv) Diagonals bisect each other: If diagonals intersect at a point and bisect each other, then triangles formed are congruent. This gives parallel opposite sides. Hence, ABCD is a parallelogram. Conclusion: Any one of the given conditions ensures that ABCD is a parallelogram.

questions.question: The common ratio of a geometric sequence is 3 and the 4th^{th}th term is 9. What is the first term of the sequence?‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: Common ratio (r) = 3 4th term (a4_44‚Äã‚Äã) = 9 Concept Used: ‚Äãan=a√ór(n‚àí1)a_n = a \times r^{(n-1)}an‚Äã=a√ór(n‚àí1)‚Äã Solution: For the 4th term: a4=a√ó3(4‚àí1) 9=a√ó33 9=a√ó27 a=927 =13a_4 = a \times 3^{(4-1)}\\ \ \\9 = a \times 3^3\\ \ \\9 = a \times 27\\ \ \\a = \frac{9}{27}\\ \ \\= \frac{1}{3}a4‚Äã=a√ó3(4‚àí1) 9=a√ó33 9=a√ó27 a=279‚Äã =31‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If tan Œ∏ = 34\frac 3443‚Äã‚Äã and Œ∏ is an acute angle, find the value of cosecŒ∏.
questions.explanation: Given tan Œ∏ = 34\frac 3443‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Œ∏ is an acute angle Formula Used ‚Äãtan‚Å°Œ∏=OppositeAdwacentHypotenuse2=Opposite2+Adwacent2cosec Œ∏=HypotenuseOpposite\tan \theta = \frac{\text{Opposite}}{\text{Adwacent}} \\\text{Hypotenuse}^2 = \text{Opposite}^2 + \text{Adwacent}^2\\\text{cosec } \theta = \frac{\text{Hypotenuse}}{\text{Opposite}}\\tanŒ∏=AdwacentOpposite‚ÄãHypotenuse2=Opposite2+Adwacent2cosec Œ∏=OppositeHypotenuse‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution Based on tan Œ∏, Opposite side = 3 and Adwacent side = 4 Using Pythagoras theorem: Hypotenuse = 32+42=9+16=25=5 \sqrt{3^2 + 4^2} = \sqrt{9 + 16} = \sqrt{25} = 532+42‚Äã=9+16‚Äã=25‚Äã=5‚Äã‚Äã cosec Œ∏ = 53 \frac{5}{3}35‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: 19 consecutive natural numbers are given in ascending order. The average of the last 7 numbers is 25. What is the average of all 19 numbers?
questions.explanation: Given: Total numbers = 19 consecutive natural numbers Average of last 7 numbers = 25 Formula Used: For consecutive numbers Average = Middle term Solution: Let the 19 consecutive numbers be arranged in order. The last 7 numbers are also consecutive, so their average = middle number of those 7 numbers. =&gt Average of last 7 numbers = 25 =&gt Middle number of last 7 numbers = 25 Now, the 7 numbers have 3 numbers before and 3 after the middle: So, last 7 numbers are: 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28 Thus, the full 19 numbers will end at 28. So, the first number = 28 ‚àí 18 = 10 Hence, the 19 numbers are from 10 to 28. The average of 19 consecutive numbers = middle number Middle position = 10th term 10th term = 10 + 9 = 19 Final Answer: Average of all 19 numbers = 19

questions.question: Find the units digit in the product of (127)153√ó(341)89(127)^{153}√ó(341)^{89}(127)153√ó(341)89‚Äã.
questions.explanation: Given: Find the units digit of (127)153√ó(341)89(127)^{153} \times (341)^{89}(127)153√ó(341)89‚Äã‚Äã Concept Used: Units digit of 7n7^n7n‚Äã follows a 4-term cycle: 7, 9, 3, 1 Units digit of 1n=11^n = 11n=1‚Äã always Solution: Units digit of 71537^{153}7153‚Äã‚Äã The units digit of powers of 7 cycles every 4: 71=7(units digit 7)72=49(units digit 9)73=343(units digit 3)74=2401(units digit 1)75=16807(units digit 7)(cycle repeats)7^1 = 7 \quad (\text{units digit } 7) \\7^2 = 49 \quad (\text{units digit } 9) \\7^3 = 343 \quad (\text{units digit } 3) \\7^4 = 2401 \quad (\text{units digit } 1) \\7^5 = 16807 \quad (\text{units digit } 7) \quad \text{(cycle repeats)}71=7(units digit 7)72=49(units digit 9)73=343(units digit 3)74=2401(units digit 1)75=16807(units digit 7)(cycle repeats)‚Äã The cycle is 7, 9, 3, 1. ‚Äã153√∑4=38 cycles with remainder 1(since 153=4√ó38+1)153 \div 4 = 38 \text{ cycles with remainder } 1 \quad (\text{since } 153 = 4 \times 38 + 1)153√∑4=38 cycles with remainder 1(since 153=4√ó38+1)‚Äã‚Äã The remainder 1 corresponds to the first position in the cycle, which is 7. Multiplying the two units digits: 7 √ó1=7.\times 1 = 7.√ó1=7.‚Äã‚Äã Units digit of 189=11^{89} = 1189=1‚Äã‚Äã Final Product Units digit =7 √ó 1 = 7 Alternate Method: Units digit of 71537^{153}7153 ‚Äã153‚àí1=152153-1 =152153‚àí1=152 152√∑4=38cycles\div 4 = 38 \text{cycles}√∑4=38cycles remainder = 0 we have subtracted 1 in the number thus now 0 + 1 = 1 So, 7¬π = 7 unit digit of 71537^{153}7153‚Äã‚Äã Units digit of 189=11^{89} = 1189=1 Final Product Units digit =7 √ó 1 = 7 ‚Äã

questions.question: The annual instalment (in ‚Çπ) to discharge a debt of ‚Çπ8,432 due in 4 years at 16% simple interest per annum is:
questions.explanation: ‚Äã Given: Debt (P) = ‚Çπ8,432 Time (T) = 4 years Rate of interest (R) = 16% per annum Formula Used: The formula to calculate the annual instalment (I) to discharge a loan with simple interest is: I = (p √ó 100) / [ ( n √ó 100) +{(n-1) + (n-2) +......+1 } √ó R] p = principal n= number of years R =rate of interest in % I = annual installment Solution: I=8432√ó100(4√ó100)+((4‚àí1)+(4‚àí2)+(4‚àí3))√ó16I=‚Çπ8,432√ó100400+(3+2+1)√ó16I=‚Çπ8,432√ó100400+96I=‚Çπ8,432√ó100496=‚Çπ1,700\text{I} = \frac{8432 \times 100}{\left(4 \times 100\right) + \left( (4-1) + (4-2) + (4-3) \right) \times 16}\\ \text{I} = \frac{‚Çπ8,432 \times 100}{400 + \left(3+2+1\right) \times 16}\\\text{I} = \frac{‚Çπ8,432 \times 100}{400 + 96}\\\text{I} = \frac{‚Çπ8,432 \times 100}{496} = ‚Çπ1,700\\I=(4√ó100)+((4‚àí1)+(4‚àí2)+(4‚àí3))√ó168432√ó100‚ÄãI=400+(3+2+1)√ó16‚Çπ8,432√ó100‚ÄãI=400+96‚Çπ8,432√ó100‚ÄãI=496‚Çπ8,432√ó100‚Äã=‚Çπ1,700‚Äã‚Äã Alternate Solution:‚Äã Formula Used: Installment Price =100√óA100√ót+rt(t‚àí1)2\frac{100\times A}{100\times t + \frac{rt(t-1)}{2}}100√ót+2rt(t‚àí1)‚Äã100√óA‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã where, A = amount, r = rate of intetest, and t = time period Solution: Putting the values in the formula, we get Installment Price =100√ó8432100√ó4+(4√ó16)(4‚àí1)2\frac{100\times 8432}{100\times 4 + \frac{(4\times 16)(4-1)}{2}}100√ó4+2(4√ó16)(4‚àí1)‚Äã100√ó8432‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Installment Price = 1700

questions.question: Evaluate: 31.752‚àí(4.98+8.0112)√∑1431.752-(4.98+8.0112)√∑\frac{1}{4}31.752‚àí(4.98+8.0112)√∑41‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given Expression: 31.752‚àí(4.98+8.0112)√∑1431.752 - (4.98 + 8.0112) \div \frac{1}{4}31.752‚àí(4.98+8.0112)√∑41‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution Step 1: Solve Bracket: 4.98 + 8.0112 = 12.9912 Step 2: Division (Dividing by ¬º is equivalent to multiplying by 4): ‚Äã12.9912√∑14=12.9912√ó4=51.964812.9912 \div \frac{1}{4} = 12.9912 \times 4 = 51.964812.9912√∑41‚Äã=12.9912√ó4=51.9648‚Äã‚Äã Step 3: Subtraction: 31.752 - 51.9648 = -20.2128 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A solid copper sphere of diameter 42 cm is melted and converted into a wire having a length of 28 cm. Then the diameter of the wire is:
questions.explanation: Given: Diameter of copper sphere = 42 cm Length of wire (cylinder) = 28 cm Formula Used: Volume of sphere = 43√óœÄ√ór3\frac{4}{3} √ó \pi √ó r^334‚Äã√óœÄ√ór3‚Äã‚Äã Volume of cylinder (wire) = œÄ√óR2√óh\pi √ó R^2 √ó hœÄ√óR2√óh‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Radius of sphere (r): 422\frac{42}{2}242‚Äã‚Äã = 21 cm When melted and recast, the volumes remain equal. ‚ÄãœÄ√óR2√ó28=43√óœÄ√ó213 R2√ó28=43√ó21√ó21√ó21 R2√ó28=4√ó7√ó21√ó21 R2√ó28=28√ó441 R2=441\pi √ó R^2 √ó 28 = \frac{4}{3} √ó \pi √ó 21^3 \\ \ \\R^2 √ó 28 = \frac{4}{3} √ó 21 √ó 21 √ó 21\\ \ \\R^2 √ó 28 = 4 √ó 7 √ó 21 √ó 21\\ \ \\R^2 √ó 28 = 28 √ó 441\\ \ \\R^2 = 441œÄ√óR2√ó28=34‚Äã√óœÄ√ó213 R2√ó28=34‚Äã√ó21√ó21√ó21 R2√ó28=4√ó7√ó21√ó21 R2√ó28=28√ó441 R2=441‚Äã‚Äã R = 21 cm The diameter of the wire: 2 √ó 21 = 42 cm Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A fair die is rolled. What is the probability of getting a number greater than 4?
questions.explanation: Given: Total outcomes (die) = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6} (n=6) Event E: Number > 4 Formula Used: P(E) = Favorable OutcomesTotal Outcomes \frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}{\text{Total Outcomes}}Total OutcomesFavorable Outcomes‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Favorable outcomes are {5, 6} (n=2). Probability = 26=13 \frac{2}{6} = \frac{1}{3}62‚Äã=31‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: If x + y = 45 and xy = 135, what is the value of x¬≤ + y¬≤ ?
questions.explanation: Given: x + y = 45 xy = 135 Formula Used: ‚Äã(x+y)2=x2+y2+2xy(x + y)^2 = x^2 + y^2 + 2xy(x+y)2=x2+y2+2xy‚Äã‚Äã Solution: ‚Äãx2+y2=(x+y)2‚àí2xyx^2 + y^2 = (x + y)^2 - 2xyx2+y2=(x+y)2‚àí2xy‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äãx2+y2=(45)2‚àí2√ó135x^2 + y^2 = (45)^2 - 2 √ó 135x2+y2=(45)2‚àí2√ó135‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äãx2+y2=2025‚àí270x^2 + y^2 = 2025 - 270x2+y2=2025‚àí270‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äãx2+y2=1755x^2 + y^2 = 1755x2+y2=1755‚Äã‚Äã Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)
